pages.raw_text:
Editor, Press-In Fridays edi-
ton you publish under the head
lines, Employment Fraud Again, :"
one-sided article regarding a
couple of carpenters whom / sent
to work and who returned with
out securing it. The mere fact of
these men having returned without
having secured the promised en-
ployment does not necessarily
brand it as fraudulent transac-
ton. If you have space for an ex-
PLANTATION l will be glad to have you
say for me that these men were
delayed en- route to the work for
five days on account of boat be-
ing disabled, which was no fault of
theirs or mine. When they finally
arrived they were too late, as other
men had been picked up and put to
work. The general foreman sent
them back, stating there was no
work for them. They reported at
The Press office upon their return,
presumably counting notoriety Tor
aiming to give me some, most like.
ly the latter then called at our Of
fee and made demands for a set.
Element, which was promptly met
and they went their way. Yours
truly, Lyons Employment agency,
per A. G. Lyons.
